title: glycine hirticaulis subsp. leptosa
intro: Glycine hirticaulis subsp. leptosa, also known as the hairy soybean or glycine, is a type of perennial legume native to China. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of glycine hirticaulis subsp. leptosa include reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and digestion. In traditional Chinese medicine, this plant is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and it may also be used in combination with other herbs to create herbal preparations such as infusions and tinctures.
